description_long: This object is a vintage-style tabletop radio receiver with a wooden exterior, featuring a rich, reddish-brown wood grain finish. The front panel displays a prominent speaker grille with vertical slats on the left, a rectangular black display or tuning window on the right, and two round black control knobs below the display. The back side has ventilation slots and cutouts for internal access, as well as a visible power cord with a two-prong plug. The sides and top are smoothly contoured, with the wood grain pattern continuing seamlessly around the body. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th century radios, combining both decorative and functional elements.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a speaker grille, tuning window, and control knobs, finished in a reddish-brown wood grain. The back features ventilation slots and a power cord.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, black display window, and two control knobs, all set in a polished wood panel.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the smooth, continuous wood grain surface with the power cord partially visible at the rear.
description_view_2: The rear view displays ventilation slots, two large cutouts, and the attached power cord with a plug.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the uninterrupted wood grain surface, with the power cord emerging from the back.
